title: Planning Backed Dogfooding Guardrail
goal: Make broad planned or autopilot work visibly require an active checked-in planning record before implementation.
Preserve narrow direct execution for small tasks while preventing roadmap-backed lane work from looking like the default direct path.
non_goals: Do not block ordinary narrow fixes.
Do not turn GitHub issues into the canonical execution state.

intent.outcome: Expose a compact execution-readiness guardrail so agents dogfood Agentic Workspace planning before broad implementation work.
intent.constraints: The guardrail must be advisory and machine-readable, must preserve cheap direct work, and must route broad roadmap/autopilot work into active planning before code changes.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest summary/report/preflight surface that makes the unsafe no-active-plan state explicit.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the fix requires prompt parsing, a hard runtime gate, or a second planning authority.

execution.next_step: Archive this completed plan, close issue #322, then commit and push the guardrail.
execution.proof: Focused CLI/report/summary tests, lint, maintainer-surface checks, contract tooling checks, and planning-surface checks show the guardrail appears when roadmap candidates exist without an active execplan.

invariants: Checked-in planning remains the canonical execution substrate for broad planned work.
Narrow direct work remains allowed when no active plan exists.
Summary/report outputs stay compact and machine-readable.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: make this dogfooding failure harder to repeat by surfacing a planning-backed execution-readiness guardrail

required_continuation.activation trigger: open a new follow-up only if agents can still run broad roadmap/autopilot work from chat or issues without an active planning record

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: A compact warning and recommendation path for no-active-plan broad work.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Hard enforcement, prompt classification, and complete remediation of older lower-trust closeouts.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The current report default of `stay-direct` is unsafe when roadmap candidates exist and the user asks for broad planned implementation.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: summary exposes `execution_readiness`, report routes roadmap-backed no-active-plan work to `promote-before-broad-work`, and focused regression tests cover the behavior.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: none for this slice after review; hard commit/closeout enforcement remains optional follow-up if the advisory guardrail is bypassed again.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: decide whether commit/closeout tooling should refuse broad-lane closure without active planning residue if advisory output is insufficient

intent_interpretation.literal request: use the serious dogfooding miss as a valuable improvement signal
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: turn the operator failure into a product guardrail so the package is harder to bypass during planned work
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: surface execution readiness for roadmap-backed no-active-plan states and recommend plan promotion before broad work
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Reject a fix that only documents the mistake without changing the package's compact operating outputs.

execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "execution_shape or dogfooding or summary"; uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "summary or roadmap"; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before changing plan file formats or adding a hard runtime command gate
execution_bounds.stop before touching: unrelated memory routing, installer lifecycle, or broad issue-closeout repair

stop_conditions.stop when: the guardrail is visible in compact outputs and focused tests prove the broad/no-active-plan distinction
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: the fix needs prompt classification or runtime enforcement instead of state-derived readiness
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: the work turns into a general workflow engine or historical closeout cleanup
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: summary/report cannot expose the guardrail without breaking existing direct-work semantics

context_budget.live working set: summary/report execution-shape code, planning summary roadmap counts, focused tests, active plan state
context_budget.recoverable later: full history of the previous lane implementation run and older lower-trust closeout warnings
context_budget.externalize before shift: guardrail fields, validation commands, and any deferred hard-enforcement follow-up
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: dogfooding usage and recurring-failure notes when deciding posterity capture
context_budget.tiny resumability note: make no-active-plan plus roadmap candidates recommend promote-before-broad-work, not stay-direct
context_budget.context-shift triggers: before changing schema shape or after tests reveal incompatible summary/report expectations

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: make package dogfooding unavoidable enough for broad planned work
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: state-derived, compact, advisory, no scheduler, narrow direct work stays cheap
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: field names, exact wording, and whether the guardrail lives in summary, report, or both
delegated_judgment.escalate when: hard enforcement, prompt classification, or historical issue remediation becomes necessary

active_milestone.scope: Add state-derived execution-readiness guidance for roadmap-backed work when no active execplan exists.

immediate_next_action: Add and test the compact guardrail in summary/report outputs.
invariants: Do not represent roadmap candidates as active execution state.
Do not make direct execution impossible for small tasks.
Do not rely on chat memory for broad work sequencing.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: No-active-plan plus roadmap candidates is not enough authority for broad planned/autopilot implementation.
The compact package output should say to promote a planning record before broad work.
Direct execution remains acceptable only for narrow work that does not claim roadmap lane progress.
open_questions_to_close: Closed: the guardrail appears in planning summary as `execution_readiness` and in workspace report as the no-active-plan execution-shape recommendation.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "execution_shape or summary"
uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "summary or roadmap"
completion_criteria: No-active-plan plus roadmap candidates produces a compact promote-before-broad-work guardrail.
Existing planning-backed and narrow direct execution recommendations remain coherent.
Focused tests cover the new guardrail.
Issue #322 is closed only after proof and planning residue are current.

execution_run.executor: single-agent direct with checked-in active plan
execution_run.what happened: Added a state-derived `execution_readiness` contract to planning summaries and changed workspace report execution-shape guidance so roadmap-backed no-active-plan work recommends promotion before broad implementation.
execution_run.scope touched: .agentic-workspace planning state and execplan; planning summary implementation; workspace report implementation; focused workspace/planning tests; recurring-failure memory note.
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "execution_shape or summary or roadmap_backed"; uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "summary or roadmap"; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json; uv run ruff check src packages/planning/src tests/test_workspace_cli.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; make maintainer-surfaces; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q; uv run ruff check src packages/planning/src tests packages/planning/tests
execution_run.result for continuation: Issue #322 is satisfied by the advisory guardrail and durable anti-trap memory entry; hard enforcement should be a separate follow-up only if bypass recurs.
execution_run.next step: archive plan, close #322, commit, and push

finished_run_review.config compliance: followed active planning before implementation and used configured proof/check surfaces before closeout
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; the fix changes machine-readable operating outputs rather than only documenting the mistake
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: close #322; open hard-enforcement follow-up only if advisory readiness is bypassed again

proof_report.validation proof: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "execution_shape or summary or roadmap_backed"; uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "summary or roadmap"; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q; uv run ruff check src packages/planning/src tests packages/planning/tests; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; make maintainer-surfaces
proof_report.proof achieved now: The compact summary now exposes `execution_readiness`; roadmap-backed no-active-plan states recommend `promote-before-broad-work`; active planned work reports `planning-backed`; and focused plus broader tests pass.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: 243 targeted tests passed, ruff passed, planning surfaces were clean, contract tooling had no drift, maintainer surfaces were clean, and live summary/report output was planning-backed for this slice.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Use the serious dogfooding miss as a valuable improvement signal.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The product now surfaces the unsafe no-active-plan state directly and memory records the trap with a test/tooling remediation path.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Agentic Workspace should be a quiet repo-native continuity and execution layer that lowers restart, handoff, review, and proof cost.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer a compact queryable guardrail over more prose ritual.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does the package now make bypassing checked-in planning less attractive for broad planned work?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: the package now gives agents a compact state-derived warning before broad roadmap/autopilot work without active planning, and the active work itself was executed from checked-in planning.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: issue #322, this archived execplan, `execution_readiness` summary output, workspace report execution-shape tests, and recurring-failure memory note
closure_check.reopen trigger: agents can still implement roadmap lanes from chat without active planning residue

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Added a compact planning-backed execution-readiness guardrail for roadmap-backed no-active-plan work.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused and broader tests, ruff, summary/report live checks, planning-surface check, contract tooling check, and maintainer-surface check passed.
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: none required; optional hard enforcement only if bypass recurs
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): Memory recurring-failure note plus manifest routing; package summary/report outputs carry the operational guardrail.

drift_log: 2026-04-26: Created from the observed failure where broad roadmap lane implementation used package reports/checks but did not keep an active planning record.
2026-04-26: Completed by adding `execution_readiness`, report promotion guidance for roadmap-backed no-active-plan work, focused tests, and recurring-failure memory capture.
